speaker: interviewer
text: Fella, who is sitting next to you?

speaker: interviewee
text: It's my husband, Ben. He's been my husband for 52 years. He's helping me out, because in case I don't tell the truth. [PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] We met when we both were still children. I was 16, and he was 19. We didn't know what we were getting into, but it worked out OK, because ignorance is sometimes the best. [SIGHS] We have four children, eight grandchildren. And [INAUDIBLE]. And we have lots of [INAUDIBLE]. So we might have started out badly, but Ben is pretty good. So there were some rewards in life.

speaker: interviewer
text: Is there something you want to say to your husband, or your children, or everybody?

speaker: interviewee
text: [SIGHS] Well, what can I say? I don't know what it has to do with the past. But we're looking to a healthy old age. And I hope my children understand if we did things wrong while we were parenting, it's because we didn't know any better. We had no [NON-ENGLISH] to-- to follow.

speaker: interviewer
text: So we did the best we thought we did. And if we-- if we made mistakes, it's not because we didn't love them, but because we didn't know any better. And now that they are parents, they understand. You go by instinct, mostly, and especially us. Do you have something to say to her, Mr. Stern?

speaker: interviewee
text: Well, I'll just add perhaps that we must have done things right, because the kids are wonderful. So we just didn't have the-- the guidance from our parents. But the-- the Lord, whoever, was good to us.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah. [SIGHS]

speaker: interviewee
text: Good.

speaker: interviewee
text: Who's in this picture, Mrs. Stern? This is my grandmother-- my mother's mother.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was her name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Eta.

speaker: interviewer
text: And her last name?

speaker: interviewee
text: Eta Frost. And I am with her. I think I was about between 8 and 10 at the time. I don't remember that picture. I got it from Israel. My mother's sister lives there, and she had it. [SIGHS] So I am not sure of the circumstances when it was taken. But I was happy that she had some pictures, because I know my son would have had no recollection of what they even looked like. [INAUDIBLE] was taken already, [? right. ?] OK, [INAUDIBLE]. Who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this picture, from left to right, it's me. And I think I'm about 12 years old here. [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] My grandmother, Eta Frost. In the back is my mother, who was standing, because she was expecting at the time, sort of hiding her-- and next to her is her father and my grandfather, Shia Frost. And where was this picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture was taken in Wlodzimierz [INAUDIBLE]. That's the city we-- I was born in and we lived in. And I think all of them were born there.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. OK Who is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is my husband. He's been married to me for years, and it shows. [LAUGHS]

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was the picture taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: The picture was taken in Germany.

speaker: interviewee
text: Stuttgart.

speaker: interviewee
text: In Stuttgart.

speaker: interviewer
text: Do you remember when you took these pictures?

speaker: interviewee
text: Not really. But I think he was traveling a lot. He probably took it to send me a picture so I wouldn't forget what he looked like between travels.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who is this?

speaker: interviewee
text: That's me. This was taken in 1945. I was visiting somebody in Lodz, Poland. [PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] I can't remember the reason for-- for that picture. But it's there.

speaker: interviewer
text: And who's this?

speaker: interviewee
text: This is me, Freda Stern, and my daughter, Bella, on the way to Canada. Was on the boat called General [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewer
text: In what year?

speaker: interviewee
text: In 1950. It must have been the beginning of May.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. Who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this picture is my family, all my children, and my son-in-laws. It's at my grandson's bar mitzvah. From left to right is my son, Norman, my son-in-law Yankie, and his wife, Susan-- my youngest daughter. Next to her is Anne, my middle daughter, and her husband, Charlie. And my oldest daughter, Bella, and Mark Greene. And in front of them is me and my husband.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was it taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: It was taken in San Francisco.

speaker: interviewer
text: In what year?

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE] in--

speaker: interviewee
text: [INAUDIBLE].

speaker: interviewee
text: I would say in 80--

speaker: interviewer
text: OK. And who's in this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: In this picture are our children and grandchildren. From left to right is Liana, our granddaughter, who lives in Toronto, and the daughter of our youngest daughter. Next to her is Ezra, our oldest daughter's [INAUDIBLE], and her-- her oldest child. Next to him-- is that Susan?

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yeah, Susan, our youngest daughter, and her son. Next to her is my husband and his granddaughter, Maadi. Next to him is me, [? Fela ?] Stern. Next to me is my daughter, Anne Marmar, my granddaughter, Rachel Greene, her brother and my grandson, David Greene. Next to him is Daniel Marmar. Above him is Eli Marmar, next to his father, Charlie Marmar. Our cousins [INAUDIBLE] and Sally. And next to them is my son Norman. Next to him is his oldest sister, Bella, her husband, Mark Green. And--

speaker: interviewee
text: That's it.

speaker: interviewee
text: And next to him is--

speaker: interviewee
text: You've said it already. it was Yankie.

speaker: interviewee
text: Yankie. Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what was the occasion for this picture?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was David's bar mitzvah. That was '90--
